name: Chicken Breasts Stuffed W/garlic, Ginger, Ham & Tarragon
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound Ham, finely chopped (about one cup)
2 to 3 Garlic cloves, peeled and finely chopped
2 Tablespoons Fresh ginger, peeled and finely chopped
4 Tablespoons Fresh tarragon, chopped (or preserved in vinegar)
Lemon zest and juice or white wine
Vegetable mirepoix: 1 cup Chicken stock, 1 cup Whipping cream
recipeInstructions:
1. To prepare chicken breasts, place between sheets of waxed paper. Gently pound with a mallet until flat and tender. Sprinkle each with salt, pepper, and lemon juice. Keep covered in the refrigerator.
2. To make stuffing, combine ham, garlic, ginger, half the tarragon, and about 2/3 of the lemon zest in a bowl, blending well. Add a dash of lemon juice or wine to moisten.
3. To stuff breasts, place equal amounts of filling on the center of each breast. Bring sides up to encase the filling, shaping with your hands into a round package and tucking in ends to seal.
4. Grease the shiny side of six foot-long strips of foil, about 2 inches wide. Encircle each breast with foil, greased side in, pulling tightly so that foil wraps around the breast (This retains shape during baking). Fold over edge on top to secure foil.
5. Brush tops of chicken with butter. Sprinkle with salt, pepper, and a little tarragon. Refrigerate, covered if baking is to be done several hours later.
6. Preheat oven to 400°F. Place vegetable mirepoix in the bottom of a large baking dish. Place chicken breasts on top of vegetables. Pour enough wine around the bottom of the pan to cover mirepoix.
7. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes, then turn heat down to 350 degrees. Baste chicken and cover pan loosely with foil. Bake another 15 minutes or until chicken breasts are done but still moist inside.
8. Remove foil covering from pan; turn broiler on to brown chicken tops about 2 minutes. Remove pan from oven. Place chicken on a heated platter and keep warm. Carefully remove foil strips.
9. Meanwhile, remove and discard mirepoix vegetables from roasting pan. Place pan on top of the stove. Remove any surface fat and bring to a boil. Add chicken stock and cream and reduce over high heat, stirring until desired consistency. Taste and adjust seasonings.
10. To serve, garnish chicken breasts with remaining lemon zest and tarragon. Place a little sauce on a heated plate, then place chicken breast on top.
11. Good served with rice-stuffed tomatoes.
12. Serves 4 to 6, depending upon size of chicken breasts.
